heading: Availability Zones and Supported Regions
body:
Atlas supports the following Azure (Microsoft Azure) regions and
[availability zones](microsoft-azure-availability-zones).
While all of the following regions support dedicated clusters (`M10+`),
some regions don't support Free clusters (formerly known as `M0`)
or Flex clusters.
The Atlas API uses the corresponding
**Atlas Region**.> **Note:** Clusters spun up in an Atlas organization created through Azure (Microsoft Azure) Native
> Integration aren't limited to the specific Azure (Microsoft Azure) region where the organization
> is based. Clusters can be deployed in any of the 40+ worldwide Azure (Microsoft Azure)
> regions in which Atlas is supported. The organization region is only required
> to fit the Azure (Microsoft Azure) resource requirements and has no impact on the regions where
> clusters in the organization are deployed.> **Important:** We recommend that you use the regions marked by an asterisk
> (asterisk) in the following table as a secondary disaster
> recovery (DR) region only in a [multi-region cluster](manual/reference/glossary) because
> these regions cost higher than the other regions in the table.
>
> Also, these regions might not be available in your Azure (Microsoft Azure) environments
> without approval from Azure (Microsoft Azure) support. If you want to leverage private
> networking options, such as [VPC/VNet peering](vpc-peering) or
> [private endpoint connections](private-endpoint), with a
> cluster deployed in one or more of these regions, you must allow
> your Azure (Microsoft Azure) subscription to create resources in these regions. To
> learn more, contact [Azure support](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/).**Americas:**Americas| Azure Region    | Location                | Atlas Region       | Free Support | Flex Support | M10+ Support | Availability Zones |
| --------------- | ----------------------- | ------------------ | ------------ | ------------ | ------------ | ------------------ |
| centralus       | Iowa, USA               | US\_CENTRAL        |              |              |              |                    |
| eastus          | Virginia (East US)      | US\_EAST           |              |              |              |                    |
| eastus2         | Virginia, USA           | US\_EAST\_2        |              |              |              |                    |
| northcentralus  | Illinois, USA           | US\_NORTH\_CENTRAL |              |              |              |                    |
| westus          | California, USA         | US\_WEST           |              |              |              |                    |
| westus2         | Washington, USA         | US\_WEST\_2        |              |              |              |                    |
| westus3         | Arizona, USA            | US\_WEST\_3        |              |              |              |                    |
| westcentralus   | Wyoming, USA            | US\_WEST\_CENTRAL  |              |              |              |                    |
| southcentralus  | Texas, USA              | US\_SOUTH\_CENTRAL |              |              |              |                    |
| brazilsouth     | Sao Paulo, Brazil       | BRAZIL\_SOUTH      |              |              |              |                    |
| brazilsoutheast | Rio de Janeiro, Brazil  | BRAZIL\_SOUTHEAST  |              |              |              |                    |
| canadaeast      | Quebec City, QC, Canada | CANADA\_EAST       |              |              |              |                    |
| canadacentral   | Toronto, ON, Canada     | CANADA\_CENTRAL    |              |              |              |                    |
| chilecentral    | Santiago                | CHILE\_CENTRAL     |              |              |              |                    |

| ---------------- | -------------------------- | -------------------- | ------------ | ------------ | ------------ | ------------------ |

| southafricawest  | Cape Town, South Africa    | SOUTH\_AFRICA\_WEST  |              |              |              |                    |Atlas supports [NVMe storage volumes](nvme-storage) and [Extend Storage Capacity and IOPS on Azure](change-storage-capacity-azure) in the following Azure (Microsoft Azure) regions.**Americas:**Americas| Azure Region    | Location                | Atlas Region       | NVMe Support | Extended Storage/IOPS |
